key_themes: GLP-1 / tirzepatide franchise dominance with record Mounjaro + Zepbound sales (> $36B in 2025)
Strong 2026 guidance ($80-83B revenue, $33.50-35 EPS) beating Street estimates
Foundayo (orforglipron) oral GLP-1 approval and launch expanding obesity market
Consistent analyst buy ratings and high price targets ($1,200–$1,500+ vs. ~$920–$930 current price)
Pipeline momentum and manufacturing scale despite competitive noise from Novo
sources: 15+ recent news articles (Forbes, Motley Fool, Reuters, Yahoo Finance, BioPharma Dive, Fierce Pharma, company filings)
25+ X posts (semantic + keyword searches covering earnings reactions, Foundayo launch, analyst notes, and competitive comparisons)
summary: Eli Lilly maintains strongly bullish investor and analyst sentiment. Its GLP-1 drugs drove ~45% revenue growth in 2025, and the company issued 2026 guidance well above consensus. The fast-tracked FDA approval of oral Foundayo in early April further expands the addressable market, though first-week prescription data (≈1,390 TRx) trailed Novo’s oral launch and a preprint raised questions about comparative lean-mass loss. These near-term points have not dented the dominant narrative of sustained high growth, margin expansion, and pipeline optionality; most analysts see 30-40%+ upside from current levels around $920–$930.[[1]](https://www.forbes.com/sites/greatspeculations/2026/04/14/lly-stock-to-2000/)[[2]](https://www.fool.com/investing/2026/04/07/great-news-for-eli-lilly-stock-investors/)[[3]](https://www.tikr.com/blog/eli-lilly-stock-in-2026-fda-approval-glp-1-momentum-and-a-44-valuation-upside)[[4]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/LLY/history/)
